Claude Mythos Preview runs autonomous multi-stage cyberattack simulations, alarming UK regulators  Claude Mythos Preview is raising serious concerns in the cybersecurity world. It has demonstrated the ability to autonomously carry out multi-stage cyberattack simulations, mimicking real-world intrusion scenarios step by step. This means the AI can identify and exploit vulnerabilities at scale, something that was previously limited to highly skilled human attackers. As a result, UK regulators are becoming increasingly cautious about its potential misuse. While the technology could strengthen defensive security testing, it also highlights how advanced AI systems are rapidly changing the threat landscape.

Purple Team security exercises — where defenders watch real attack techniques run safely against their own systems — are now recognised for professional development credits by GIAC and ISC2. The technique maps attacks to MITRE ATT&CK and tests whether existing security tools actually detected them. Russia's Forest Blizzard just demonstrated something every security team needs to internalize: you don't need malware to own a network.  APT28 modified DNS settings on 18,000+ SOHO routers, mostly end-of-life Mikrotik and TP-Link devices. No code deployed. No payloads. Just a DNS reconfiguration that redirected OAuth token flows to attacker-controlled servers.  200+ organizations compromised. 5,000+ consumer devices caught in the dragnet. Microsoft, Lumen's Black Lotus Labs, and the UK's NCSC all confirmed it.  The attack surface wasn't software. It was infrastructure nobody was monitoring. How many end-of-life routers are on your network right now? How many branch offices run equipment that hasn't seen a firmware update since 2022?  The next generation of threat intelligence doesn't start at the endpoint. It starts at the infrastructure layer most organizations forgot they had.
Heads up APT28's SOHO router DNS hijack campaign makes QR codes riskier than ever. A benign-looking QR can resolve to an attacker site so always preview links before scanning. #QRsecurity #DNSHijack

The news release is an official FBI/NSA public service announcement from April 7, 2026. Russian GRU (military intelligence, aka APT28/Fancy Bear) cyber actors have been exploiting vulnerable small-office/home-office (SOHO) routers worldwide—often via known flaws like in TP-Link models—to hijack DNS traffic and steal sensitive data like credentials, emails, and intel from connected devices.  It's true: The US DOJ/FBI, with 21 international partners, just disrupted a large GRU-controlled network of these routers in a court-authorized op called "Masquerade." They targeted them indiscriminately because home/small business routers are cheap entry points—weak defaults, outdated firmware, exposed to the internet—letting attackers proxy attacks and siphon traffic from high-value targets like military/gov users without direct hacks.   Update firmware, change defaults, disable remote access, and reboot to protect yourself. Full PSA here: ic3.gov/PSA/2026/PSA260407

...At least keep it current.  Try subscribing to CISA alerts. "The operation, linked to the cyber actor APT28 (also known as Fancy Bear or Forest Blizzard), has targeted devices since at least 2024, compromising TP-Link and MikroTik routers using known vulnerabilities...

We published a full breakdown of this operation on Monday. APT28 changed the DNS settings on 18,000 MikroTik and TP-Link routers across 120 countries. No malware deployed.   They just redirected DNS queries for Microsoft authentication domains to attacker-controlled servers and silently harvested Office 365 tokens from 200+ organizations.   A reboot alone is not enough. You need to verify your router's DNS settings are pointing to your ISP or a known resolver like 1.1.1.1 or 8.8.8.8.   If the DNS was changed, a reboot does not undo that. Factory reset and firmware update is the actual fix.

sample_tweets.text: 2026년 4월 14일, 오전 9:49 EDT 제목: 미국 재무부, 취약점 발견을 위해 앤스로픽의 '미토스' 접근 권한 요청  미국 재무부 기술팀이 사이버 보안 취약점을 조사하기 위해 앤스로픽(Anthropic)의 새로운 AI 모델 '미토스(Mythos)'에 대한 접근 권한을 추진 중입니다. 앤스로픽은 기업들이 미토스를 사전에 테스트하고 방어망을 구축하지 않을 경우 사이버 공격에 악용될 수 있다고 강력히 경고했습니다.  이에 따라 재무부와 연방준비제도(Fed)는 최근 월스트리트 리더들을 소집해 미토스로 인한 사이버 위험에 적극 대비할 것을 촉구했습니다. 미 국방부가 앞서 앤스로픽을 공급망 위험 기업으로 지정했음에도 불구하고 재무부는 접근을 요청하고 있으며, JP모건, 골드만삭스 등 주요 월가 은행들은 이미 내부적으로 해당 모델을 테스트하고 있습니다.

Question: mythos가 모든 보안을 뚫을정도로 막강하다고 하던데 그러면 비트코인이나 암호화폐도 뚫을 수 있는가?  아니요, Mythos(AI 모델)는 비트코인이나 암호화폐의 핵심 보안을 완전히 뚫을 수 없습니다. Mythos는 소프트웨어 코드의 취약점(제로데이)을 찾아 공격 코드를 자동으로 만드는 데 매우 뛰어나지만, 비트코인의 ECDSA 서명·SHA-256 해싱 같은 수학적 암호학 기반은 AI가 코드 분석만으로 깨뜨릴 수 없기 때문입니다. 다만 지갑·노드·거래소·DeFi 스마트 컨트랙트 같은 구현(implementation) 부분의 버그는 빠르게 찾아내 해킹할 가능성이 높아, 실질적인 피해 위험이 커집니다.  암호화폐는 AI를 위해 만들어진 보안 시스템?

■ 미토스가 다른 이유 기존 AI는 사람이 침투 경로를 지시해야 했다. 미토스는 다르다 🤖 취약점 분석부터 공격 코드 제작까지 스스로 완수. 격리된 가상 환경을 스스로 탈출하고 활동 흔적까지 지웠다. 해킹 재현 평가 83.1% 박사급 추론 시험 56.8% — AI 최초 50% 돌파.

트럼프 행정부 관계자들이 은행들에게 앤트로픽의 미토스(Mythos) 모델 테스트를 권장하고 있습니다.  앤트로픽의 미토스를 실제 사이버 방어 도구로 활용하라는 것입니다. 앤트로픽은 미토스가 제로데이 취약점(아직 패치가 되지 않은 버그)을 찾아내고 악용하는 데 매우 뛰어난 성능을 보이는 범용 모델이라고 밝혔습니다.  또한 앤트로픽은 미토스가 주요 운영 체제와 브라우저 전반에 걸쳐 결함을 발견했기 때문에, 광범위하게 배포하는 대신 프로젝트 글래스윙(Project Glasswing)을 통해 접근을 제한했다고 설명했습니다.  영국에서도 규제 당국과 국가 사이버 보안 기관이 미토스가 은행, 보험사, 거래소에 어떤 위험을 초래할 수 있는지 논의하고 있습니다.  SK텔레콤

개인정보위는 2026년 공공기관 개인정보 보호수준 평가계획을 확정했습니다.  올해부터는 공공기관 유출사고와 부실 대응에 대한 패널티를 대폭 강화합니다. 사고가 나면 더 크게 감점하고, 대응이 미흡한 경우 추가로 책임을 묻겠습니다.  평가 지표도 바뀝니다. 모의해킹과 취약점 점검 실적 등 ‘사전 예방’ 지표를 새롭게 도입하고, 기관장의 개인정보 보호 노력에 대한 평가 비중도 확대했습니다. 사고 이전에 얼마나 준비하고 관리했는지를 보다 면밀히 평가하겠습니다.  현장 지원도 확대합니다. 6월부터 권역별 설명회를 열어 평가 기준을 충분히 안내하고, 컨설팅이 필요한 기관에는 1:1 맞춤형 현장 컨설팅을 제공해 실질적인 개선을 뒷받침하겠습니다.  보호수준 평가는 중앙행정기관, 지방자치단체, 공공기관, 지방공기업, 교육행정기관 등 총 1,464개 기관을 대상으로 실시합니다. 공공부문 전반을 아우르는 이번 평가를 통해 책임 있는 관리체계가 현장에 안착하도록 하겠습니다.  #개인정보보호위원회 #개인정보위 #PIPC #공공기관보호수준평가 #사전예방

Modern disk wipers have evolved beyond simple MBR corruption to target GPT metadata, NTFS structures, and leverage signed drivers via BYOVD techniques. Understanding these progression stages is critical for DFIR triage and recovery planning.  Evolution breakdown: • Legacy: MBR-only corruption (WhisperGate) - leaves file data recoverable via TestDisk/carving • Advanced: GPT + NTFS $MFT targeting (HermeticWiper, CaddyWiper) using IOCTL_DISK_SET_DRIVE_LAYOUT_EX • Evasive: BYOVD approach loading legitimate signed drivers (EaseUS via \\.\EPMNTDRV\%u device path) • Simplified: File-level overwrites (BiBi, Hatef, Hamsa) with 4KB blocks for cross-platform speed  Iranian 🇮🇷 threat actors increasingly favor simpler file-level destruction over complex disk manipulation for operational speed across mixed Windows/Linux environments.  Monitor for \\.\PhysicalDrive0 access, unexpected driver loads, partition layout IOCTLs, and USN Journal corruption patterns. Each technique closes different recovery paths - scope the method early to determine realistic restoration options.  #DFIR_Radar

🔍 DFIR Suite API Spotlight: URLScan[.]io Deep Analysis  The POST /phishing/urlscan endpoint gives you comprehensive URL analysis without ever touching the link in your browser.  Submit a suspicious URL and get back the full picture: page screenshot, DOM content, complete HTTP transaction chain, linked resources, and indicators of malicious JavaScript or phishing kits. Perfect for analyzing that sketchy link from a phishing email or investigating a reported URL without burning your own infrastructure.  Real-world scenario: Your SOC gets a ticket about a credential harvesting campaign. Users are clicking links in convincing emails. You grab one of the URLs, POST it to /phishing/urlscan, and within moments you have the rendered page screenshot, the full redirect chain, embedded scripts, and any third-party resources being loaded. You identify the phishing kit, trace the infrastructure, and feed IOCs into your detection stack — all without visiting the page yourself.  Key details: • Full request/response forensics for each HTTP transaction • Identifies JavaScript obfuscation and redirect patterns • 3 credits per request • Available on all plans  This is the kind of tooling that turns a vague "suspicious link" ticket into actionable threat intelligence in minutes.  #DFIR #DFIRSuite #ThreatIntel

AI-generated detection rules in seconds.  You've just triaged a phishing campaign. You have IOCs, TTPs, a rough understanding of the attack chain — but now you need detection rules. Writing Sigma, YARA, or Suricata from scratch takes time you don't have.  The DFIR Suite /ai/detect endpoint bridges that gap. Submit your threat context (IOCs, behaviors, file characteristics) and get back deploy-ready detection rules tailored to your environment. It generates Sigma rules for log-based detection, YARA for file hunting, or Suricata for network traffic — complete with logic explanations and false positive guidance.  This isn't about replacing detection engineers. It's about accelerating the first draft so you can focus on tuning and validation. Feed it a malware sample's behavior, get a Sigma rule targeting process creation and registry changes. Describe a C2 pattern, get a Suricata signature.  15 credits per request. POST to /ai/detect with your threat intel, specify your rule type, and iterate from there. Threat intelligence becomes actionable detection content in minutes, not hours.  #DFIR #DFIRSuite #API

🚨 OSINT in the Wild: Recruiter Outreach Pattern  Received a recruiter email this morning for OSINT / Cyber Threat Intelligence roles.  At first glance, it appeared legitimate.  On closer review:  • Gmail address used alongside an Apple recruiter signature • Multiple email domains listed (@apple.com + @gmail.com) • Generic role bundling (OSINT Analyst / CTI Associate / Research Specialist) • No specific company, req ID, or hiring team • Vague “partnership with leading organizations” language  Individually, none of these are definitive.  Together, they form a pattern.  This is how many scam or funnel-based approaches operate: ➡️ Broad targeting ➡️ Weak identity anchoring ➡️ Gradual trust building before escalation  Assessment: Low-confidence legitimacy → disengaged  Tradecraft takeaway: In OSINT and threat intelligence, the signal is rarely one indicator—it’s the convergence of small inconsistencies.  After 30+ years in environments where details matter, one principle holds: Small deviations are often the first signal—not the last.  Stay sharp.  #OSINT #ThreatIntelligence #CyberSecurity #FraudPrevention #SocialEngineering #ScamAwareness #RiskAnalysis #DueDiligence #InfoSec #AnalystMindset

The "Claude Mythos" hype wasn't even a month old before OpenAI decided to remind the world who owns the frontier.  They just launched GPT-5.4-Cyber.  On the surface, it’s a cybersecurity model. It can reverse-engineer binaries and hunt vulnerabilities. But look closer at the how, and you'll see the real story.  OpenAI is introducing "Trusted Access."  To get the most powerful version of this model, you can't just pay a subscription. You have to go through ID verification via Persona. You have to prove who you are to be "trusted" with the tool.  This is a tectonic shift in how AI is distributed.  We are moving from "Software as a Service" to "Permissioned Intelligence."  OpenAI is effectively becoming the gatekeeper of high-tier capabilities. They are deciding who is "legitimate" enough to access tools that could either save a company from a breach or dismantle its entire infrastructure in seconds.  For the business owner, the lesson is clear:  The "democratization of AI" was a honeymoon phase. We are now entering the era of Stratified Intelligence.  There will be the AI for the masses (the chatbots) and the AI for the "Trusted" (the power tools). The gap between the two will be the new competitive moat.  If you think you're just "using a tool," you're missing the point. You are navigating a new hierarchy of power.

It is not a traditional remote control tool but instead attacks iOS systems when a user visits a link via Safari.  Attack stages:  ▪️ Stage 1 - Browser Attack (Stage1 terrorbird / Stage1 cassowary) ▪️ Stage 2 - PAC Bypass (Stage2 seedbell) ▪️ Stage 3 - Kernel Privilege Escalation (Stage3 Variant A / Stage3 Variant B)  Capabilities:  ▪️ Break through system isolation and access all data on device ▪️ Theft of cryptocurrencies and bank information ▪️ Integrated HTTP vulnerability web server and C2 remote control server ▪️ Attack link auto-generated after server startup ▪️ Link delivered via social engineering, AirDrop, or other methods (one-click via Safari) ▪️ Connected device management panel ▪️ Operator console with real-time logging  The panel UI is in Chinese with a 5-step operational workflow: modify configuration, start services, set up connection, wait for callback, and control device. The actor notes that the two iOS 26 versions have already been patched. Proxy mode is available for earning dividends.

🇫🇷 Zadig & Voltaire Data Leak Claimed – 500K+ Records  A threat actor is advertising an alleged database belonging to Zadig & Voltaire, a Paris-based fashion brand, claiming over 500,000 records exposed.  📊 Alleged Data Includes: • First and last names • Email addresses • Gender information  🧠 Threat Intelligence Insight: • Typical customer database leak structure, likely sourced from: •E-commerce platform compromise •Third-party marketing/CRM exposure • Dataset size suggests broad customer impact, not limited to internal users  ⚠️ Potential Risks: • Targeted phishing campaigns using brand impersonation • Credential stuffing attacks (email reuse scenarios) • Increased fraud risk leveraging customer profiling  📊 Status: Unverified — monitoring for confirmation and data validation  💬 Retail and fashion brands continue to be prime targets due to high-volume customer data and monetization potential.  #CyberSecurity #DataLeak #ThreatIntel #DarkWeb #RetailSecurity #DailyDarkWeb

This is very good malware.  This is solid-solid-SOLID B+ malware, very close to A- malware.  APT37 is using a old-school playbook. They're doing EPO (Entry Point Obfuscation) on a self-delivered binary for evasion. They also unironically are using something akin to cavity infection ... but on themselves. This is something you saw more in the Windows 95 - Windows XP era, not something you see in 2026.  Very cool. I respect it.   The multi-staged fragmentation of shellcode phases is also really, really, really cool. This is (once again) a more old-school technique usually reserved for infected binaries, not self-delivered binaries.  Despite all of these super cool features, APT37 shoots themselves in the foot immediately.  - EAT walking for Kernel32 functionality (???) - XOR decryption is a huge red flag - Allocating with PAGE_EXECUTE_READWRITE (???) - Hardcoded OAuth token (???) - Used external dependency for AES (???)  Why not use NT functionality to hook evasion? XOR is easily identified in static analysis, why XOR? Allocating memory with VirtualAlloc with RWX is a MASSIVE RED FLAG. They also hardcode a OAuth token ... they can multi-staged shellcode payload with old-school malware techniques but hardcore AN OAUTH TOKEN?  It unironically makes me wonder if they had one old-head malware guy working on it, then they had some newer dude do the non-hardcore stuff. There is a huge gap in skill sets here.  Or the old-head hasn't kept up to date on malware stuff since 2005... or they got lazy... I don't know, really weird.
